ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301638 covers hot chocolate as a food product; this record is a sweetened malt-chocolate variant. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers cocoa and chocolate-based beverages; this product includes malt. Closest defensible FoodEx2 category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT code identified for sweetened hot chocolate malt drink; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

preparation_method: dissolved in hot water or hot milk

serving.description: 1 prepared cup (approximately 200 ml, made with water)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 200 ml of prepared hot chocolate malt drink made with hot water, equivalent to about 200 g.

nutrient_source.title: Hot Chocolate Malt Drink — Singapore HPB Foodo Database (G00010956)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo record G00010956 for HOT Chocolate Malt Drink. All core macros and micronutrients sourced from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this item; set to -1. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat not reported; set to -1.

gi_source.title: Cocoa-based sweetened beverage — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened chocolate malt drinks (e.g., Milo, GI ~34–55 range; sweetened cocoa beverages GI ~55)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No exact GI entry found for this specific HPB product. GI estimated at 55 based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened chocolate malt beverages and cocoa-based drinks. Milo (a comparable sweetened malt chocolate drink) has a reported GI of approximately 34–55 depending on preparation. A conservative mid-range value of 55 is used. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 20.72) / 100 = 11.4 for a 200 g serving. GI 55 is classified as 'low' according to University of Sydney GI Database criteria (GI <= 55 is low).

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk; sodium is low at 3 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sweetened beverage as its sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) exceeds the 5 g/100 g beverage sugar threshold and added sugar independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines, despite the very low sodium (3 mg/100 g); an unsweetened cocoa preparation is preferred.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 9.94 g/100 g with zero dietary fibre in a liquid form causes rapid glucose absorption and blood glucose spikes
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this drink as it contains 9.94 g sugar per 100 g with no dietary fibre, making it a sugar-sweetened beverage that causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; if a warm chocolate drink is desired, unsweetened cocoa powder prepared with low-fat milk and no added sugar is recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) with negligible beneficial nutrients; regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased CVD risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit this drink due to its high sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) and absence of fibre, omega-3 fatty acids, or other cardioprotective nutrients; regular sugar-sweetened beverage consumption is independently associated with increased CVD risk and should be replaced with unsweetened alternatives.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (9.94 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; zero dietary fibre provides no gut microbiome support for immune regulation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this sweetened drink as its high sugar content promotes inflammatory mediators and it lacks anti-inflammatory components such as omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols in material quantity, or dietary fibre; an unsweetened dark cocoa preparation would provide flavonoid benefit without the inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hot Chocolate Malt Drink (热巧克力麦芽饮料) is a sweetened instant beverage popular in Singapore hawker centres, cafes, and food courts. It is prepared by dissolving a pre-mixed powder containing cocoa, malt extract, and sugar in hot water or hot milk. The resulting drink is warm, sweet, and mildly chocolatey with a characteristic malt undertone. As sold and recorded in the Singapore HPB Foodo database (G00010956), the prepared beverage contains approximately 42 kcal per 100 g, with 10.36 g carbohydrate (of which 9.94 g is sugar), 0.25 g fat, and negligible protein (0 g by formulation). Dietary fibre is absent (0 g), and sodium is very low at 3 mg/100 g. The drink is classified as ultra-processed due to its instant powder format with added sugar and flavourings. Clinically, this is a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high sugar content and absence of fibre, which results in rapid glucose absorption. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous sweetened chocolate malt beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database. A 200 ml serving delivers approximately 11.4 glycemic load units. For those seeking a warm chocolate drink, an unsweetened cocoa powder preparation with low-fat milk and no added sugar is a clinically preferable alternative.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo database record G00010956 at confidence 0.95. This is an exact match from the primary approved regional source for Singapore foods. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database due to absence of an exact product entry, which slightly reduces GI confidence but does not affect overall record confidence given the strong nutrient provenance.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo G00010956; this is a dilute sugar-and-malt beverage with no protein-contributing ingredients in material quantity — confirmed zero by formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source. magnesium_mg is confirmed zero (0.0 mg) per HPB source. saturated_fat_g set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo record G00010956. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB Foodo for this item. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo G00010956 and not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific Singapore commercial product. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened chocolate malt beverages; no exact product GI entry found. Food group corrected from input 'Grains and staples / Baking ingredients' to 'Beverages / Hot chocolate and malt drinks'. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) per G6 and Q8 requirements. GI 55 is classified as 'low' according to University of Sydney GI Database criteria (GI <= 55 is low).
